world_date: Month of the Amber Silence, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord
major_event: Elowen's messenger returns from Frostvale bearing Caeris Thorne's personal signet — an offer of secret alliance against the Shadow Court. The Glenhaven-Thorne axis, if real, would redraw every war map in Aeloria.

recent_events.text: Elowen's messenger returned from Frostvale carrying Caeris Thorne's personal signet ring. Elowen has not shown it to her war-council. She has not denied receiving it either.
Kessa Stonebraid was found at dawn standing at the sealed mine-gate below the antechamber, palm flat against the stone, symbols on her arms glowing faintly. She did not wake when spoken to.
Roderic summoned his naval advisors in private. Three Tidefall warships remain in Eldoria harbor. He has drafted — but not signed — an ultimatum demanding their withdrawal within thirty days.
Pirate King Rowen launched two vessels westward under no flag. Arkenor's vanished commander was confirmed aboard one. Their heading, by last sighting, is toward the Tidefall outer shipping lanes.
The Grand Druid's Conclave concluded in closed session. The novice was removed from the scriptorium and placed in the Sealed Garden. His maps now cover its inner walls floor to ceiling.

active_tensions.description: Elowen's receipt of Caeris's signet suggests a covert alliance forming. Lythara has informants in Frostvale — if she learns of this, Arkenor will not wait for orders.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Roderic vs Tidefall vs Aldric
active_tensions.description: The unsigned ultimatum is a trap or a test. Aldric knows of its drafting. Tidefall ships have not moved. The harbor is now effectively an occupied position.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld Dwarves — Deep Entity vs Surface
active_tensions.description: Kessa's trance at the sealed gate suggests the entity below is escalating contact. The pulse is faster. The antechamber unsealing was not a resolution — it was an invitation.

active_tensions.factions: Rowen + Arkenor Commander vs Tidefall Shipping Lanes
active_tensions.description: Two unflagged Dreadwind ships moving toward Tidefall lanes threatens the Admiral's economic lifeline. Rowen is leveraging Arkenor's exile for his own sea-war.

character_updates.name: Sovereign Elowen Silverleaf
character_updates.status: Holds Caeris's signet in private. Has begun quietly redeploying war-bands — not to the Faerwood border, but northward along the ridge paths toward Frostvale.

character_updates.name: Prince Caeris Thorne
character_updates.status: Still in Frostvale. His false name has cracked — the council knows who he is and has not expelled him. He has sent his signet south and is awaiting Elowen's formal reply.

character_updates.name: Kessa Stonebraid
character_updates.status: Found in a trance at the sealed mine-gate. Symbols on forearms now extend to her neck. When she finally woke, she asked only: 'Has it opened yet?' No gate had moved.

character_updates.name: King Roderic Thorne II
character_updates.status: Drafted an ultimatum to Tidefall but has not signed it. He is using its existence as leverage — someone is meant to discover it before he decides whether to send it.

character_updates.name: Prince Aldric Thorne
character_updates.status: Learned of the ultimatum draft through a court clerk. Made no visible move. That evening he sent a coded courier north — toward Frostvale, not Tidefall. A second board is opening.

character_updates.name: Pirate King Rowen
character_updates.status: Two ships launched under no flag toward Tidefall lanes. Rowen himself remains on the Islands, publicly visible — plausible deniability worn like a coat.

faction_morale.reason: Lythara's court celebrates Arkenor's success but senses her attention has shifted. She is watching Frostvale with an intensity that unsettles even Clan Velorn.
The return of the messenger with Caeris's signet has filtered into rumor among the war-bands. They do not know the contents — but Elowen is moving them north, and that is enough.
The court knows Roderic drafted something he will not sign. Indecision at the throne fractures confidence. Aldric's silence reads as patience — or readiness.
Ships in harbor, compact dissolved on paper but not in practice. The Admiral is winning without fighting. An unsigned ultimatum is not a threat — it is an invitation to wait.
Kessa's trance and the accelerating pulse have broken something in the loyalist ranks. Three senior foremen have requested transfer to upper-level tunnels. Ironmaul refused.
The novice's confinement to the Sealed Garden has not stopped the map-etching. The Conclave ended without consensus. The Grand Druid has stopped eating.
Rowen's gambit with Arkenor's commander electrifies the pirate fleet. Action after seasons of exile — even veiled action — is enough to restore faith in their king.
Internal investigation into the glass-dart leak has narrowed to three suspects. No one has been named. The Syndicate is still watching the orc war with careful distance.

whispers.text: Caeris's signet was not sent freely. The Frostvale council required it as collateral before they would let him leave the mountain. He is still a guest — or a hostage.
The symbols on Kessa Stonebraid's arms are not dwarven. A blind lore-keeper in Stonebreak recognized three of them — they are the same as markings on the novice's maps.
Rowen's two ships carry no cargo and no soldiers. Only instruments. The kind used to measure sea-floor depth — someone is mapping what lies beneath the Tidefall lanes.
Aldric's courier to Frostvale carries a mirror-sealed letter. The seal is not a Thorne mark. It is a glyph associated with the Dur Khadur Dark Council.

weather_and_omens.condition: The twin moon-shadow has fused back into one — but it moves now independently of the moon itself. It drifts. Clan Arkenor has barred their doors three nights running.
The pulse has reached 45 beats. The pauses have vanished entirely. It is no longer a rhythm — it is a sustained, low vibration that can be felt in the teeth.
Snow fell upward for one hour at midday. The flakes did not return. The Frostvale sentinels have begun sleeping in shifts — no one will admit why.
The Sealed Garden's inner walls are warm to the touch despite the cold season. The novice sleeps twelve hours, wakes for two, and maps. He no longer speaks.
A flock of starlings formed a signet-ring shape above Eldoria palace and held it for six minutes before dispersing. The court astrologer resigned the same afternoon.

absorbed_lore: Elowen's messenger returned from Frostvale bearing Caeris Thorne's personal signet ring — a secret offer of alliance against the Shadow Court.
Elowen has begun redeploying war-bands northward along ridge paths toward Frostvale rather than toward the Faerwood border.
Caeris Thorne's false name has been seen through by the Frostvale council, who have not expelled him — his signet may have been collateral, not a gift.
Kessa Stonebraid was found in a trance at the sealed mine-gate, symbols now extending to her neck; she woke asking if the gate had opened.
The Lostfeld pulse has escalated to a continuous vibration — the countdown cadence has collapsed into sustained resonance.
Roderic drafted but did not sign a Tidefall ultimatum — using its existence as intelligence bait.
Aldric learned of the ultimatum and sent a courier to Frostvale bearing a Dur Khadur glyph seal — opening a third political board.
Rowen launched two flagless ships carrying depth-measuring instruments toward Tidefall shipping lanes — a cartographic operation, not a raid.
Arkenor's vanished commander is confirmed aboard one of Rowen's ships, cementing the Shadow Court–Dreadwind covert link.
The novice at Stonebreak no longer speaks and maps continuously; the Sealed Garden walls are warm despite winter.
Kessa's forearm symbols match markings on the novice's maps — confirmed by a blind lore-keeper in Stonebreak.
The Gloomspire internal investigation into the glass-dart leak has narrowed to three suspects within the Syndicate.
